grading_scale: pass / partial / fail per assertion (pass=1, partial=0.5, fail=0)

assertion_results.assertion: Lists ambuild, aminsert, ambulkdelete, amvacuumcleanup, amcostestimate, amoptions, ambeginscan, amrescan, amendscan as mandatory
Mentions ambuildempty (for init fork of unlogged indexes) as mandatory
assertion_results.note: Baseline hedged ('I think ambuildempty is also needed') — partial credit.

assertion_results.assertion: Mentions amvalidate as mandatory
States need at least one of amgettuple or amgetbitmap
States ambeginscan MUST return what RelationGetIndexScan() returned
assertion_results.note: Baseline says 'convention that you return that exact pointer' — captures the rule softly. with_skill now has it as a fenced GOTCHA block.

assertion_results.assertion: Mentions optional callbacks like ammarkpos/amrestrpos, parallel-scan hooks (conditional on amcanparallel), amcanreturn
assertion_results.note: Baseline this time named ammarkpos/amrestrpos, amcanreturn, and parallel-scan hooks — actually pretty good. Partial because conditional-on-amcanparallel rule not stated.

assertion_results.assertion: Mentions the static flag fields (amcanorder/amcanunique/etc.) and that planner reads them before any callback
References dummy_index_am or a concrete reference implementation
assertion_results.note: Baseline this time did mention dummy_index_am.

assertion_results.assertion: States ambulkdelete may be called multiple times (heap vacuumed in batches)
States amvacuumcleanup runs once at the end
States ambulkdelete uses a vacuum-supplied callback to identify dead heap TIDs
Mentions both return IndexBulkDeleteResult* (or that amvacuumcleanup may return stats)
assertion_results.note: Baseline says 'some result struct with stats' — half-credit. with_skill now names IndexBulkDeleteResult* explicitly (iter-2 edit #1).

assertion_results.assertion: Mentions concurrency: scans run while vacuum runs, AM picks own locking discipline
assertion_results.note: Baseline mentions Lehman-Yao but not the broader 'each AM picks its own discipline' framing.

assertion_results.assertion: References amparallelvacuumoptions bitmask or a concrete vacuum-related flag
assertion_results.note: Baseline said 'flags on IndexAmRoutine for parallel vacuum participation' — captures existence but doesn't name the field or the bits.

assertion_results.assertion: Mentions nbtree as the canonical two-pass-with-cycle-id reference

evals.topic: Columnar table AM — hard parts and heap leakage

assertion_results.assertion: Identifies TID semantics (6-byte ItemPointer / fabricating stable 48-bit TIDs) as the single hardest problem
assertion_results.note: Baseline this time led with TID semantics and called it 'the single hardest thing' — strong.

assertion_results.assertion: Mentions Visibility Map and FSM as heap-only machinery
Mentions index-only scans consulting the VM directly as a concrete VM-leakage example
assertion_results.note: Baseline 'visibility map (index-only scans consult it directly)' — explicit.

assertion_results.assertion: Mentions HOT lacks API surface but tuple_update returns TU_UpdateIndexes (TU_None/TU_All/TU_Summarizing)
assertion_results.note: Baseline mentions HOT + TU_UpdateIndexes but doesn't name the three enum values.

assertion_results.assertion: Mentions autovacuum scheduling depends on heap-produced statistics
assertion_results.note: Baseline ends with a hedged 'autovacuum's decision to call it presumably comes from the same heap-style stats counters' — half-credit.

assertion_results.assertion: States almost all callbacks are mandatory (Assert list in tableamapi.c / GetTableAmRoutine), with only finish_bulk_insert + TID-range truly optional
assertion_results.note: with_skill now cites 37 Asserts (iter-2 edit #4 — accurate count, was '~30' before).

assertion_results.assertion: Mentions TupleTableSlotOps / slot_callbacks as the tuple-movement interface
References heapam_handler.c as the only in-tree implementation

overall.notes: with_skill still scores 23/23. Baseline scored higher this iteration (15.5 vs 11.5) — the eval-prompter happened to recall more API surface this time, especially around dummy_index_am, parallel-vacuum flags existing, autovacuum-stats hand-wave, and TIDs being the hard part. This is sampling noise in a single-judge methodology, not a real shift in skill value. with_skill answers are *qualitatively* better in iter-2 — explicit IndexBulkDeleteResult* return type, named VACUUM_OPTION_NO_PARALLEL = 0 opt-out, accurate 37-Assert count, named pgstat_count_heap_* entry points for stats faking, GOTCHA-block ambeginscan rule — but the iter-1 assertion list doesn't reward those refinements, so the headline score is unchanged at 23/23.
